task.prompt: Write a function `flatten(xs)` that flattens a nested list into a single flat list.

gold_spec.goal: Recursively flatten a list of arbitrary depth into a single-level list, preserving left-to-right depth-first order of leaves.

inputs.name: xs
inputs.description: list whose elements may themselves be lists

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: single-level list of all leaves of xs, in left-to-right depth-first order

postconditions: no element of result is itself a list
the relative order of leaves matches a left-to-right depth-first traversal of xs
edge_cases: fully flat list returns a copy with same elements in same order
tuples are NOT treated as nested; they are leaves
strings are NOT treated as nested; they are leaves
None elements are preserved as leaves
behavior at extreme nesting depth (>1000 levels) is unspecified — may hit Python's recursion limit
